Operational Responsibility: Application team owns OS patching, runtime installation, NGINX, disk, backup, monitoring agent and VM hardening
Migration Fit: Best for lowest-change rehost when the application cannot be containerized or needs OS-level access
Scaling Model: Manual resize, VM Scale Sets or scripted horizontal scale; app/runtime must be made scale-safe
Ingress And TLS: Requires NGINX, Load Balancer, Application Gateway or Front Door in front of the VM; TLS certificate lifecycle is team-owned unless delegated to App Gateway/Front Door
Networking And Egress: Strong VNet, subnet, NSG and route-table control; private endpoints and firewall patterns are possible but must be designed and operated
Identity And Secrets: Managed identity and Key Vault are available, but app integration and secret rotation patterns are team-owned
Release And Rollback: Rollback usually means redeploying VM artifacts, restoring backup/snapshot or switching traffic back to the old VM
Observability: Azure Monitor VM insights, Log Analytics agent and custom app logs require explicit configuration
Cost Model: Always-on compute, managed disk, backup and monitoring costs even when traffic is low
Key Trade-Off: Maximum control and fastest rehost path, but highest operational burden and least modernization benefit
Recommendation For Spring PetClinic: Useful as Module 4 rehost baseline; not preferred for Module 7 because the app is already containerized

Operational Responsibility: Platform manages host OS, web front end, TLS endpoint and common web-app operations; team manages image, app settings and scaling rules
Migration Fit: Good for straightforward web containers that need simple PaaS hosting, deployment slots and minimal platform engineering
Scaling Model: Instance-count scale-out and autoscale rules; no Kubernetes-style event scaling
Ingress And TLS: Built-in HTTPS, custom domains and managed certificates; supports private endpoints and Front Door integration
Networking And Egress: VNet integration for outbound private access; less granular container-network control than AKS
Identity And Secrets: Managed identity, app settings and Key Vault references are mature and simple
Release And Rollback: Deployment slots support blue-green style swaps and quick rollback to previous image/slot
Observability: App Service logs, metrics and Application Insights integration are simple to enable
Cost Model: Always-on App Service Plan cost; predictable but can be less efficient for bursty or low-traffic workloads
Key Trade-Off: Simpler than Container Apps for classic web apps, but less container-native for revisions, sidecars and event scaling
Recommendation For Spring PetClinic: Viable fallback if the assessor values deployment slots and classic PaaS operations over Container Apps revision features

Operational Responsibility: Azure manages the container environment, ingress, revisions, replicas and KEDA-based scaling; team manages image, secrets, app config and target network choices
Migration Fit: Best fit for this module: containerized monolith with managed ingress, revision traffic, secrets and scaling without AKS cluster operations
Scaling Model: KEDA-driven min/max replicas, scale-to-zero capable when configured, and revision-level traffic control
Ingress And TLS: Built-in external or internal HTTPS ingress with custom domain/TLS support; can sit behind Front Door or Application Gateway/WAF
Networking And Egress: Managed environment can use VNet integration; egress controls can be layered with NSGs, firewall, NAT Gateway and private endpoints
Identity And Secrets: Managed identity and Container Apps secrets are supported; Key Vault integration can be added for production-grade secret flow
Release And Rollback: Revision model supports controlled rollout, traffic splitting and rollback to a prior healthy revision
Observability: Container App metrics, logs, revisions and replica health integrate with Log Analytics
Cost Model: Consumption or workload-profile based pricing; can be cost-efficient for variable traffic while still supporting always-on minimum replicas
Key Trade-Off: Less cluster-level control than AKS, but much lower platform operations burden
Recommendation For Spring PetClinic: Recommended Module 7 target because it satisfies managed container, ingress, secrets, revisions and scaling requirements with balanced operations

Operational Responsibility: Azure manages the control plane, but the team owns node pools, upgrades, ingress controller, cluster security, policies, add-ons and Kubernetes operations
Migration Fit: Best for multi-service platforms needing Kubernetes APIs, service mesh, custom controllers or strict platform standardization
Scaling Model: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler, Cluster Autoscaler, KEDA and advanced scheduling; most flexible but requires cluster engineering
Ingress And TLS: Requires Ingress controller, Load Balancer, Application Gateway Ingress Controller or service mesh; TLS/cert-manager/WAF pattern must be operated
Networking And Egress: Deep CNI, NetworkPolicy, private cluster, service mesh and egress gateway patterns; strongest control and highest complexity
Identity And Secrets: Workload identity, external secrets and CSI drivers are powerful but require platform governance
Release And Rollback: Native rolling updates plus progressive delivery tools such as Argo Rollouts or Flagger; rollback is flexible but depends on platform maturity
Observability: Azure Monitor for containers, Prometheus/Grafana and centralized logging need cluster-level setup and ownership
Cost Model: Cluster/node baseline cost even for small workloads; efficient at scale when many services share the cluster
Key Trade-Off: Most powerful and portable, but operationally heavy for a single Spring Boot monolith
Recommendation For Spring PetClinic: Not recommended for this assessment app unless the enterprise already has an AKS platform team and shared cluster standards
